and let \( A_{n}=\prod_{k=1}^{n} \frac{(4 k-3) f(4 k-3)}{(4 k-1) f(4 k-1)} \). Find the value of \( A_{25} \).', initial_info={'experiment_name': 'orz57k-notool', 'task_model_id': 'bedrock/google.gemma-3-4b-it', 'episode_index': 0}, steps=[TrajectoryStep(step_index=1, action="Let $f(x) = x^5 - 3x^3 + 2x^2 + 3x + 6$.
We are given $A_n = \prod_{k=1}^n \frac{(4k-3) f(4k-3)}{(4k-1) f(4k-1)}$.
We want to find $A_{25}$.
$A_n = \prod_{k=1}^n \frac{(4k-3) f(4k-3)}{(4k-1) f(4k-1)} = \prod_{k=1}^n \frac{4k-3}{4k-1} \cdot \frac{f(4k-3)}{f(4k-1)}$.

Let's compute the first few terms of $A_n$.
$A_1 = \frac{(4(1)-3) f(4(1)-3)}{(4(1)-1) f(4(1)-1)} = \frac{1 \cdot f(1)}{3 \cdot f(3)} = \frac{f(1)}{3 f(3)}$.
$f(1) = 1^5 - 3(1)^3 + 2(1)^2 + 3(1) + 6 = 1 - 3 + 2 + 3 + 6 = 9$.
$f(3) = 3^5 - 3(3)^3 + 2(3)^2 + 3(3) + 6 = 243 - 81 + 18 + 9 + 6 = 195$.
$A_1 = \frac{9}{3 \cdot 195} = \frac{9}{585} = \frac{1}{65}$.

$A_2 = \frac{(4(2)-3) f(4(2)-3)}{(4(2)-1) f(4(2)-1)} = \frac{5 f(5)}{7 f(7)}$.
$f(5) = 5^5 - 3(5)^3 + 2(5)^2 + 3(5) + 6 = 3125 - 3(125) + 2(25) + 15 + 6 = 3125 - 375 + 50 + 15 + 6 = 2811$.
$f(7) = 7^5 - 3(7)^3 + 2(7)^2 + 3(7) + 6 = 16807 - 3(343) + 2(49) + 21 + 6 = 16807 - 1029 + 98 + 21 + 6 = 15897$.
$A_2 = \frac{5 \cdot 2811}{7 \cdot 15897} = \frac{14055}{111279} = \frac{5 \cdot 2811}{7 \cdot 15897}$.

Let's consider the case $n=1$.
$A_1 = \frac{(4(1)-3) f(4(1)-3)}{(4(1)-1) f(4(1)-1)} = \frac{1 \cdot f(1)}{3 \cdot f(3)} = \frac{f(1)}{3 f(3)}$.
$f(1) = 1 - 3 + 2 + 3 + 6 = 9$.
$f(3) = 243 - 3(27) + 2(9) + 3(3) + 6 = 243 - 81 + 18 + 9 + 6 = 195$.
$A_1 = \frac{9}{3 \cdot 195} = \frac{9}{585} = \frac{1}{65}$.

Let's consider the case $n=2$.
$A_2 = \frac{(4(2)-3) f(4(2)-3)}{(4(2)-1) f(4(2)-1)} = \frac{5 f(5)}{7 f(7)}$.
$f(5) = 5^5 - 3(5^3) + 2(5^2) + 3(5) + 6 = 3125 - 3(125) + 2(25) + 15 + 6 = 3125 - 375 + 50 + 15 + 6 = 2811$.
$f(7) = 7^5 - 3(7^3) + 2(7^2) + 3(7) + 6 = 16807 - 3(343) + 2(49) + 21 + 6 = 16807 - 1029 + 98 + 21 + 6 = 15897$.
$A_2 = \frac{5 \cdot 2811}{7 \cdot 15897} = \frac{14055}{111279} = \frac{5 \cdot 2811}{7 \cdot 15897}$.

If $A_n = \frac{1}{65}$ for all $n$, then $A_{25} = \frac{1}{65}$.
